text.title: success-metrics-definition Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for success-metrics-definition as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Too many metrics (10+ KPIs tracked on the same dashboard)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Focus diffuses; teams cannot optimize for everything. 3-5 primary KPIs force prioritization.

failure-mode.description: No North Star (multiple competing metrics treated as equally important)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Organizational misalignment; marketing optimizes for acquisition while product optimizes for retention, with no shared direction.

failure-mode.description: Vanity metrics (total signups ever, total users ever, total revenue ever)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These only go up; they don't tell you if current cohorts are healthy. Track cohort retention and weekly active instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Metrics become decorations; teams don't know if performance is good or bad. "Retention is 60%" — is that success or failure? Without a target, no one knows.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Different teams count differently; the metric becomes meaningless. Define edge cases.

failure-mode.description: AARRR framework maps poorly to B2B enterprise products with long sales cycles
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For B2B, adapt: Awareness (brand study), Consideration (demo requests), Decision (contracts signed), Adoption (usage depth), Expansion (upsell). The principle stays the same.
